skill.name: skill-development
skill.description: Guidance for creating, improving, and organizing Claude Code plugin skills with clear triggers, progressive disclosure, and reusable resources.
skill.summary: Create and refine Claude Code plugin skills with structured guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review shows they are Markdown examples and skill-writing guidance rather than executable malware. The skill has no bundled scripts, network calls, environment access, credential handling, or prompt injection text; publication risk is low because it still instructs users to create directories and run local command examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged shell-looking snippets as external command execution. These lines are documentation examples for creating skill directories, testing plugin loading, and running packaging helpers; no bundled executable code runs them automatically. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced code blocks or inline command examples in Markdown. The package contains SKILL.md and one reference file, with no executable script file present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Path References
low_findings.description: The path traversal detector flagged sibling-skill references that begin with ../. They are a study list of nearby plugin skills, not file-reading logic or instructions to access sensitive paths. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged paths appear only as Markdown bullets naming related skill directories. There is no code that resolves, opens, copies, uploads, or executes these paths.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography blockers, but review found prose about SKILL.md, descriptions, and Markdown examples rather than cryptographic APIs or hashing logic. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, encryption routines, credential storage, or signature verification. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited material is natural-language skill documentation and file-extension examples. No semantic evidence supports a real cryptography implementation or insecure crypto choice.

content.value_statement: Skill authors need clear structure so Claude and Codex load the right guidance at the right time. This skill provides a practical workflow for writing focused Claude Code plugin skills with useful resources.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides authors through use case discovery before writing a skill.
Shows when to use references, examples, scripts, and assets.
Provides writing rules for trigger descriptions and imperative instructions.
Includes validation checklists for organization, style, and resource references.
Adapts the general skill-creator method for Claude Code plugin skills.
limitations: Does not include executable validation scripts in this package.
Does not create a complete marketplace manifest or plugin package by itself.
Requires human judgment to choose useful trigger phrases and examples.
References related plugin skills by path, but does not include those skills.

use_cases.title: Create a New Plugin Skill
use_cases.description: Plan the skill purpose, create the folder structure, and draft focused SKILL.md guidance.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Skill Documentation
use_cases.description: Refine trigger phrases, move long details into references, and make instructions easier for AI agents to use.

use_cases.title: Standardize a Skill Library
use_cases.description: Apply common structure, validation checks, and progressive disclosure rules across multiple plugin skills.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Claude Code plugin skill for [task]. Identify trigger phrases, core workflow steps, and needed resources.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Skill Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SKILL.md outline for a plugin skill about [domain]. Use third-person frontmatter and imperative body instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Review Skill Organization
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my skill structure. Tell me what should stay in SKILL.md and what should move to references, examples, scripts, or assets.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Skill Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these plugin skills for trigger quality, progressive disclosure, resource references, writing style, and missing validation steps.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for validating project configuration files.
output_examples.output: A concise skill plan with trigger phrases, core validation workflow, suggested reference files, and a checklist for testing expected prompts.

output_examples.input: My SKILL.md is too long and repeats examples.
output_examples.output: A reorganization plan that keeps core workflow guidance in SKILL.md and moves long patterns, examples, and advanced notes into references.

output_examples.input: Review whether this skill is ready for a plugin release.
output_examples.output: A readiness review covering frontmatter quality, resource links, imperative writing style, examples, validation steps, and remaining gaps.

best_practices: Start from concrete user requests before writing trigger phrases or resource lists.
Keep SKILL.md focused and move detailed guidance into referenced files.
Validate that every referenced resource exists and supports a real workflow.
anti_patterns: Using vague descriptions that do not include realistic user trigger phrases.
Putting all details into SKILL.md when references would reduce context load.
Adding scripts, examples, or assets that are not documented or tested.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create and improve Claude Code plugin skills with clear triggers, focused instructions, and useful supporting resources.

faq.question: Does it generate a full plugin?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on skill content and organization inside a plugin, not full plugin scaffolding.

faq.question: When should I add a reference file?
faq.answer: Add a reference file when details are useful but too large or specialized for the always-loaded SKILL.md body.

faq.question: When should I add a script?
faq.answer: Add a script when a task needs repeatable execution, deterministic validation, or code that would otherwise be rewritten often.

faq.question: Can this help with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is written for Claude Code plugin skills and is useful when preparing skill content for Claude and Codex workflows.

faq.question: What security review result applies to this skill?
faq.answer: The reviewed package is low risk. Static findings were documentation examples, not executable malicious behavior.
